title: halophila ovalis subsp. ovalis
intro: Halophila ovalis subsp. ovalis, also known as the sea ovalis or ovalis halophila, is a species of seagrass that belongs to the family Hydrocharitaceae. This plant is found in tropical and subtropical regions of the world and has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Halophila ovalis subsp. ovalis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against oxidative stress. The herbal preparation of this plant is typically done through drying and infusing the leaves in hot water, which can be used to make tea or applied topically to the skin.
